_about: Featured survivors, hand-ordered from their PUBLIC bio summaries on the Crestwood OHP archive to give the Guided view a clean arc. These are REAL people. Per the project's ethics (docs 04/08/09), nothing here is asserted as authoritative: every waypoint is verified=false (pending) until a human checks it against the full testimony AND Mr. Masters grants written permission. Dates are taken only where the public summary states them; otherwise precision is left vague. 'as_written' preserves the survivor's/archive's own spelling alongside the canonical modern name.

survivors.name: Martin Baranek
survivors.bio_excerpt: Born in 1930 in Starachowice, Poland. Put into the Starachowice Ghetto in 1941, then deported to Auschwitz-Birkenau, sent on a death march, and liberated at Gunskirchen in spring 1945. Reached Canada in 1949.

waypoints.source_quote: born August 15, 1930 in Starachowice, Poland
put into the Starachowice Ghetto in 1941
deported to Auschwitz-Birkenau, where he stayed until the winter of 1944-5
liberated from the Gunskirchen camp by the American army in the spring of 1945
Martin spent time in Italy
made his way to Israel
emigrated to Canada in 1949

survivors.name: Israel Cohen
survivors.bio_excerpt: Born in Lodz, Poland. Survived the Lodz Ghetto, Auschwitz, and Kaufering until liberation, recovered in Switzerland, and settled in Canada.

waypoints.source_quote: born is Lodz, Poland
At first he was in the Lodz Ghetto
then Auschwitz
and then Kaufering until liberation
he went to Switzerland, to a treatment center for TB
he came to Canada, where he lives now

survivors.name: Hedy Bohm
survivors.bio_excerpt: Grew up in prewar Romania in a region that came under Hungarian control. Deported to Auschwitz in summer 1944, then a work camp; after liberation she reached Prague and arrived in Canada in 1948.

waypoints.source_quote: grew up in prewar Romania
the family was deported to Auschwitz in the summer of 1944
They were able to escape to Prague
get visas to Canada, where she arrived in 1948

survivors.name: Felicia Carmelly
survivors.bio_excerpt: Born in 1932 in Romania. She and her family were deported to Transnistria; she survived and was liberated by the Soviet Army, then travelled via Vienna and Israel before arriving in Canada in 1962.

waypoints.source_quote: Born in 1932 amidst European anti-Semitism
taken from their hometown to Transnistria … forced into mass ghettos
travelled to Vienna and Israel
travelled to Vienna and Israel before finally arriving in Canada
finally arriving in Canada in 1962

survivors.name: Josef Cipin
survivors.bio_excerpt: A young boy when the war began, on the run and hiding with partisans until the Gestapo caught up; deported to the Terezín (Theresienstadt) camp, which he survived. Remained in Czechoslovakia, then came to Canada.

waypoints.source_quote: After the war he remained in Czechoslovakia
deported to the Terezin camp, where he managed to survive
He ended up coming to Canada
